# Examples

This directory contains example outputs demonstrating how to coordinate the agency's agents on real-world tasks.

## Why This Exists

The agency-agents repository defines dozens of specialized agents across engineering, design, marketing, product, support, spatial computing, and project management. But agent definitions alone don't show what happens when you **deploy multiple agents simultaneously** in a single task.

These examples answer the question: *"What does it actually look like when the entire agency collaborates?"*

## Contents

### [nexus-spatial-discovery.md](./nexus-spatial-discovery.md)

**Content:** A complete product discovery exercise where 8 agents work in parallel to evaluate a software opportunity and develop a unified plan.

**Scenario:** Web research identified an opportunity at the intersection of AI agent orchestration and spatial computing. The entire agency was then deployed simultaneously to produce:

- Market validation and competitive analysis
- Technical architecture (8-service system design, full-SQL architecture)
- Brand strategy and visual identity
- Go-to-market and growth plan
- Customer support operations blueprint
- UX research plan including personas and journey maps
- 35-week project execution plan with 65 sprint tickets
- Spatial interface architecture specification

**Agents Used:**
| Agent | Role |
|--------|------|
| Product Trend Researcher | Market validation, competitive landscape |
| Backend Architect | System architecture, data models, API design |
| Brand Guardian | Positioning, visual identity, naming |
| Growth Hacker | GTM strategy, pricing, launch plan |
| Support Responder | Support tiers, onboarding, community |
| UX Researcher | Personas, journey maps, design principles |
| Project Shepherd | Phase plan, sprints, risk register |
| XR Interface Architect | Spatial UI specifications |

**Key Takeaway:** All 8 agents run in parallel and produce coherent, cross-referenced plans without coordination overhead. Results demonstrate the agency's ability to go from "identifying an opportunity" to "here's a complete blueprint" in a single session.

## Adding New Examples

If you conduct an interesting multi-agent exercise, consider adding it here. Good examples show:

- Multiple agents collaborating toward a shared goal
- The breadth of agency capabilities
- Practical applicability of agent definitions
